drift.note: spec says 'in progress'; live site presents Twilio as a shipped provider alongside Telnyx — spec aspiration gap if not actually live, or spec is stale.
marketing gap — live site prominently features WhatsApp Web QR flow (/admin/whatsapp-web) and 'session-backed channels'; spec doesn't mention it at all.
marketing gap — live site leads with 'Native Chat' as a top-level channel and 'Open Chat App' CTA; spec has no mention of a provider-independent native chat surface.
marketing gap — live site lists Telegram as a supported channel; spec only mentions SMS/MMS/WhatsApp.
marketing gap — live site says 'voice-ready routing' in the headline and 'voice staged next'; spec doesn't mention voice at all.
spec aspiration — spec lists WebSocket for agent delivery as a feature; live site makes no mention of WebSocket anywhere.
description mismatch — live site has an explicit 'For Personal OS' usage section calling out zmsg_bridge; spec frames zmsg as a Zerg agent gateway with no Personal OS positioning.
other — spec documents deployment details (Fly org, volume); live site has no deployment info, which is expected for a public page but worth noting if spec is the only source of truth for infra.
